commands.review_summary:
The PR wraps plugin `prependSystemContext` and `appendSystemContext` contributions from modern and legacy hooks with an attributed Markdown boundary in the harness and embedded runner, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +31, Tests +89. Total +120 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main joins plugin system-context fields and base workspace-style prompt text with blank-line separators in the harness and embedded runner helper. I did not run the live model attribution smoke in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: This active external PR should stay in human review until contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer compatibility acceptance are present; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.

target.title: fix(agents): bound plugin system context
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87045 by wrapping plugin-emitted system context with a stable boundary and attribution note before it is joined into model-visible system/developer instructions.

The wrapper is applied only at plugin hook merge points for `before_prompt_build` / legacy `before_agent_start` system context. The lower-level system prompt composer remains unchanged, so non-plugin runtime context is not mislabeled as plugin content.

## Root Cause

Plugin hook fields such as `appendSystemContext` were joined next to the base system prompt with only blank-line separation. When the base prompt ended with Codex workspace-file Markdown and the plugin emitted another `##` heading, the model could treat the plugin block as another section of the preceding workspace file.

## Behavior addressed

Plugin-provided `prependSystemContext` and `appendSystemContext` now render as a separate `# OpenClaw Plugin System Context` block bounded by `---`, with a note that the content came from OpenClaw plugins and is not part of a workspace file or project document.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Plugin hook system context no longer appears as an unbounded continuation of workspace-file Markdown.

**Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `codex/87045-plugin-context-boundary`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `8e41c118fa80c186ac40676e87bfecf988101ecb`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
```

**Evidence after fix**:

```text
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests       308 passed (308)
```

`git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` completed cleanly.

**Observed result after fix**: The harness regression test composes developer instructions ending in workspace-style Markdown followed by plugin `appendSystemContext`; the resulting instructions include `---`, `# OpenClaw Plugin System Context`, the plugin attribution note, and then the injected plugin rules.

**What was not tested**: Live model attribution smoke. Focused oxlint was attempted, but the wrapper is currently blocked before linting by unrelated plugin-sdk boundary DTS errors in current main under `src/media/*` and `src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.ts`.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Fixes #87045
target.author: monkeyleet
